hero.title: AI-native video editor
hero.subtitle: An AI-native, open-source video editor and free alternative to CapCut. No uploads, no tracking — your media stays on your device.

features.title: Everything you need to edit
features.aiNativeDesc: AI is built into every step of your workflow. Generate images, transcribe audio, create captions, and let the AI agent edit videos for you.
features.privacyFirstDesc: Your files never leave your device. All processing happens locally in your browser — no uploads, no servers.
features.worksEverywhereDesc: No installation needed. Open your browser on any platform and start editing right away.
features.openSourceDesc: Fully open source and community-driven. Inspect the code, contribute, or fork it for your needs.
features.multiTrackDesc: Professional timeline with support for video, audio, text, and sticker tracks. Drag, trim, and split with ease.
features.exportAnywhereDesc: Export your projects in MP4 or WebM format with adjustable quality settings.
features.toolsDesc: A focused set of tools designed for clarity and speed. No feature bloat — just what matters.

cta.title: Ready to start editing?
cta.subtitle: No sign-up required. Open the editor and start creating — your first project is just a click away.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about Cutia and how it works.

faq.whatIsCutiaAnswer: Cutia is an AI-native, open-source video editor that runs entirely in your browser. It is a free, privacy-first alternative to CapCut — no installation or sign-up required, just open the website and start editing.
faq.isFreeAnswer: Yes, Cutia is completely free. It is open-source software licensed under a permissive license. There are no hidden fees, subscriptions, or premium tiers.
faq.uploadsFiles: Does Cutia upload my files to a server?
faq.uploadsFilesAnswer: All your media files and editing operations stay on your device. However, AI-related features (such as AI image generation) may send data to third-party AI services or Cutia's temporary relay server for processing.
faq.exportFormats: What export formats does Cutia support?
faq.exportFormatsAnswer: Cutia supports exporting videos in MP4 and WebM formats with adjustable quality settings (low, medium, high, and very high).
faq.worksOfflineAnswer: Cutia runs in your browser and requires an initial page load. Once loaded, most editing features work without an active internet connection since all processing is done locally.
faq.differentFromCapcut: How is Cutia different from CapCut?
faq.differentFromCapcutAnswer: Unlike CapCut, Cutia is fully open source and runs entirely in your browser. Your media files stay on your device — only AI features may communicate with external services. Cutia is AI-native with built-in AI agent, image generation, and audio transcription, with no account or subscription required.
faq.isOpenSourceAnswerAlt: Yes. Cutia is fully open source and available on GitHub. You can inspect the code, contribute, fork it, or self-host it on your own server.

faq.isOpenSourceAnswer: Yes, Cutia is fully open source and community-driven. You can inspect the source code, contribute, or fork it on GitHub.
faq.isGoodAlt: Is Cutia a good alternative to CapCut?
faq.isGoodAltAnswer: Yes. Cutia is designed as a free, open-source, privacy-first alternative to CapCut. It offers AI-native editing, multi-track timeline, MP4/WebM export, and runs entirely in your browser — no account, no uploads, no watermarks.
faq.sameFeatures: Does Cutia have the same features as CapCut?
faq.sameFeaturesAnswer: Cutia covers the core editing features most creators need: multi-track timeline, text and sticker overlays, AI image generation, audio transcription, and caption generation. CapCut offers additional advanced features like effects templates and more export formats, but Cutia is rapidly growing as an open-source project.
faq.freeNoWatermarks: Is Cutia really free with no watermarks?
faq.freeNoWatermarksAnswer: Yes. Cutia is 100% free with no premium tiers, no subscriptions, and no watermarks on exported videos. It is open-source software that you can use without any restrictions.
faq.capcutUploads: Does CapCut upload my videos to servers?
faq.capcutUploadsAnswer: Yes. CapCut requires uploading your media files to remote servers for processing and storage. Cutia takes the opposite approach — all media processing happens locally in your browser and your files never leave your device.
faq.noAccount: Can I use Cutia without creating an account?
faq.noAccountAnswer: Yes. Cutia requires no sign-up or login. Just open the website and start editing immediately. Your projects are saved locally in your browser.
faq.aiFeatures: What AI features does Cutia offer compared to CapCut?
faq.aiFeaturesAnswer: Cutia is AI-native with a built-in AI agent that can edit videos from natural language prompts, AI image generation for creating visuals, and audio transcription for automatic caption generation. These features are integrated into the core editing workflow.
faq.chromebook: Can I use Cutia on a Chromebook?
faq.chromebookAnswer: Yes. Cutia runs entirely in your browser and works on any platform including Chromebooks, shared computers, and tablets — no installation or plugins required.

comparison.title: Feature-by-feature comparison
comparison.capcutPaid: Free tier with paid Pro plan

comparison.capcutPolicyTitle: CapCut Privacy Policy, Section I — Information We Collect
comparison.capcutPolicyQuote: "We may collect User Content through pre-uploading at the time of creation, import, or upload, regardless of whether you choose to save or publish that User Content."
comparison.capcutDesktop: Web version available, desktop app preferred

schema.appDescription: Cutia is an AI-native, open-source video editor that runs entirely in your browser. A free, privacy-first alternative to CapCut with AI-powered editing, multi-track timeline, and MP4/WebM export — no uploads, no tracking.
schema.browserRequirements: Requires a modern web browser with WebCodecs support
schema.aiAgent: AI agent for automated video editing
